sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 4-year-old's mother, injured her right knee while skiing in Vermont last weekend.
She caught her ski while coming off the lift and fell, resulting in a loud popping sound and persistent pain.
She was unable to bear weight and was taken down by the ski patrol.
She reports pain on the outside of her knee and a feeling that it will buckle.
She has had decreased flexion and extension, and a positive Lachman sign.
She is unable to straighten her knee fully.
She denies numbness or tingling in her toes and is able to feel her toes.
She has a history of being an active person, chasing her children, but has been hobbling on crutches since the injury.
Past medical history: The patient is otherwise healthy, with no reported high blood pressure, diabetes, or other medical conditions.
She has a supportive home environment, with her husband at home, although he works part-time.
She has twins who are 4 years old.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has edema and an effusion over the right knee, with pain to palpation of the right lateral knee.
She has decreased flexion and extension, and a positive Lachman sign.
The dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses are palpable.
There is no leg edema in the ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has a ruptured anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) in her right knee.
Problem list: ACL tear; right knee pain; decreased flexion and extension; positive Lachman sign; knee effusion; knee edema.
Plan:
I ordered a knee MRI to confirm the suspected ACL tear.
I advised the patient to continue taking ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
I also recommended wearing a knee brace to provide support to the knee.
I will schedule a follow-up appointment to discuss the MRI results and determine the next steps for treatment.
Follow-up: I will see the patient again soon to discuss the MRI results and plan for further treatment.
